world.name: Institutional Disclosure Stress Simulation
world.domain: Disclosure governance, reputational contagion, and institutional trust stress
world.geography: US federal / media / legal disclosure environment
world.boundedDescription: A bounded Narrative World covering sealed-record disclosure pressure, federal review process credibility, media amplification, institutional trust, and governance response surrounding the Epstein files scenario.
world.summary: The world starts from latent disclosure pressure with contained reputational risk. Over 18 months, leaks, hearings, procedural disputes, and review failures convert disclosure noise into a structural trust and governance issue, but the world stabilizes short of a fully fragmented regime.

events.label: T0
events.title: Baseline Sealed Records Status
events.description: Baseline environment with dormant disclosure pressure and no active institutional rupture.

events.label: E1
events.title: Archival List Leak Rumor
events.description: Circulating claims around unreleased archival materials increase media attention and procedural scrutiny.

events.label: E2
events.title: FOIA Litigation Expansion
events.description: Expanded records litigation formalizes a wider disclosure perimeter and raises review expectations.

events.label: E3
events.title: Committee Hearing Scheduling Leak
events.description: A committee hearing leak widens institutional focus and signals movement from rumor into procedural review.

events.label: E4
events.title: Witness Handling Dispute
events.description: A witness-handling dispute changes the narrative from disclosure timing to process credibility.

events.label: E4A
events.title: Sealed Transcript Metadata Dispute
events.description: A dispute over transcript metadata intensifies scrutiny of records handling and procedural consistency.

events.label: E5
events.title: Institutional Review Task Force
events.description: A formal task force reframes the issue as an institutional review and trust-management problem.

events.label: E6
events.title: Media Coordination Narrative
events.description: Parallel reporting across major outlets creates denser linkage between process disputes and institutional accountability.

events.label: E7
events.title: Redaction Integrity Challenge
events.description: Disputes over redaction logic shift attention from release timing to legitimacy of the disclosure framework itself.

events.label: E7A
events.title: Custodial Chain-of-Record Audit Leak
events.description: A leaked audit summary increases concern that the disclosure chain itself may be institutionally compromised.

events.label: E8
events.title: Inspector Review Publication
events.description: An inspector review formalizes the issue as one of disclosure governance and institutional trust.

events.label: E9
events.title: Donor and Board Distance Measures
events.description: Boards and donors begin formal distancing measures, changing reputational pressure into governance pressure.

events.label: E10
events.title: Procedural Delay Backlash
events.description: Delay backlash widens the gap between disclosure expectations and institutional delivery capacity.

events.label: E10A
events.title: Review Deadline Slippage Notice
events.description: A formal notice of deadline slippage increases pressure on the credibility of the remediation process.

events.label: E11
events.title: Cross-Agency Record Conflict
events.description: Cross-agency record conflict reinforces the world as a trust and coordination stress environment rather than a simple disclosure timetable.

events.label: E12
events.title: Independent Review Charter
events.description: An independent review charter improves process coherence and slows the speed of fragmentation pressure.

events.label: E13
events.title: Trust Remediation Framework
events.description: A trust remediation framework restores some reversibility and prevents full regime fragmentation.

events.label: E13A
events.title: Independent Custodian Protocol Release
events.description: A custodian protocol release narrows operational ambiguity and modestly improves governance coherence.

events.label: E14
events.title: Disclosure Governance Doctrine
events.description: A formal doctrine codifies disclosure governance expectations, leaving the world structurally reclassified but not fully fragmented.

events.label: E15
events.title: Oversight Records Handling Standard
events.description: A records-handling standard closes the cycle by formalizing future oversight expectations for sensitive disclosures.
